Stripe checkout, scheduled reorders, free-samples flow, a branded-product configurator with quantity tiers, and a Google Merchant feed. Trusted by 500+ UK businesses including the Mandarin Oriental, Marriott, Edwardian Hotels and Hawksmoor.

Two deliverables: a sober, project-led marketing site, and a stateless web FTP/FTPS client (CCA-FTP) so the firm could share large CAD files securely, without a desktop tool. No database — credentials live in encrypted cookies for the session only.
services_section_subtitle: Specification, design, code, deployment, monitoring, on-call. One engineer for the whole stack.
service_1_summary: Server-rendered, properly indexed, often multilingual. From editorial portfolios to multi-language real-estate sites with translated URL slugs and per-locale sitemaps.
service_1_bullet_3: PDF brochures, video feeds, CRM sync
service_2_summary: Stripe checkout, scheduled reorders, custom product configurators, free-samples flow, Google Merchant feed. From cart logic to shipping options to admin dashboard — all custom, no Shopify lock-in.
service_3_summary: Full custom web applications. Rails 8 backend, Hotwire on the front, Postgres behind. Auth, roles, audit logs, background jobs, deployment, monitoring — the boring parts done right.
service_3_bullet_3: Deployed on Kamal, monitored with Sentry
service_4_summary: The software that runs your back-office. CRMs, dashboards, sync engines, compliance workflows. KYC, sanctions screening, STRIX reporting, third-party API integration — built around your operations.
how_section_title: No tickets. No queue. No middlemen.
how_point_1: Not an account manager. Not a PM. The person writing your code is the same person you message at 9am about a bug.
how_point_2: No queues, no Slack channels to babysit, no JIRA. A short async message when something needs my eyes, a weekly written update for the rest.
how_point_3_title: Two or three clients. That's it.
how_point_3: I take a handful of clients at a time. When I'm full, I'll tell you — I won't pretend. You're never project #37.

pricing_subtitle: Flat monthly fee. Cancel any month.
pricing_feature_2: Unlimited requests, one at a time

personal_message_2: I work with Monaco SMEs because I live here and I know the local pain: software priced for global enterprises, agencies that send junior staff after they win the pitch, freelancers who disappear between projects. I don't subcontract. I don't fixed-price work I haven't scoped. I won't pretend I have capacity when I don't.

case_afida_meta_title: Afida — Custom Rails 8 B2B e-commerce | Laurent Curau
case_afida_meta_description: Custom Rails 8 e-commerce for eco-friendly packaging. Stripe, scheduled reorders, branded-product configurator, Google Merchant feed. Trusted by 500+ UK businesses.
case_afida_title: Afida — a B2B store the Mandarin Oriental orders from.
case_afida_lede: A custom-built Rails 8 e-commerce platform for eco-friendly packaging. Stripe checkout, scheduled reorders, a free-samples flow, a branded-product configurator with quantity tiers, and a live Google Merchant feed. Used daily by 500+ UK hospitality and food businesses.
case_afida_challenge_subtitle: A B2B packaging brand stuck between Shopify limits and bespoke complexity.
case_afida_challenge_1_title: Shopify couldn't model the branded-product flow
case_afida_challenge_1: Branded packaging is not a simple SKU — it's a base product + design upload + quantity tier + organisation that re-orders. Off-the-shelf carts cap out around the first two.
case_afida_challenge_2: B2B buyers don't want surprise charges. They want a heads-up email three days before each order with a link to confirm, modify, or skip.
case_afida_challenge_3_title: Real product feed, not a plugin guess
case_afida_challenge_3: Google Shopping needs `item_group_id` to cluster size variants (8oz / 12oz / 16oz). Generic plugins mangle this. The catalogue had to be modelled correctly from day one.
case_afida_solution_1: Products carry SKU, price, stock, and pack-size. ProductFamilies group related products (e.g. "Single Wall Cups" containing 8oz, 12oz, 16oz) and share an `item_group_id` for Google Shopping. Branded products extend this with design uploads and quantity tiers.
case_afida_solution_2: Stripe-hosted checkout for PCI scope, with three shipping options (standard, express, samples-only at £7.50), automatic 20% VAT, and a webhook that creates the Order on success — never on the redirect, so refresh-rage doesn't double-charge.
case_afida_solution_3: Customers pick a frequency (monthly, bi-monthly, quarterly), save a payment method via Stripe, and receive an email three days before each order. They can confirm, modify, or skip — and only the confirmed order gets charged. No surprise subscriptions.
case_afida_solution_4: Live XML feed re-rendered on every product change. Cluster `item_group_id` per family, full media URLs, availability, price. The product team uploads a photo; the feed updates within minutes.
case_afida_stack_subtitle: A modern Rails stack — chosen for shipping, not for slides.
case_afida_results_subtitle: Eighteen months live, no platform migration in sight.
case_afida_results_1_value: Mandarin Oriental · Marriott · Edwardian Hotels · Hawksmoor · The Grove
case_afida_results_3_value: Hundreds of SKUs, branded variants, free samples — one admin
case_afida_byline_title: Built and shipped by me, end-to-end.
case_afida_byline: From data model to Stripe webhooks to Google Shopping XML to the production VPS — every layer of Afida was designed and written by me. No agency middlemen, no offshore subcontracting, no junior hand-offs.
case_afida_cta_title: Need a custom store that Shopify can't model?
case_afida_cta_body: I build B2B and B2C commerce end-to-end — from data model to checkout to feed.
case_gare_meta_title: Agence de la Gare — Monaco real estate, 9 locales | Laurent Curau
case_gare_meta_description: Rails 8 multilingual real-estate site for Monaco's pioneer agency (founded 1942). 9 locales, Immotoolbox sync, Typst PDFs, multi-currency.
case_gare_title: Agence de la Gare — Monaco's 1942 agency, online in 9 languages.
case_gare_lede: A custom Rails 8 site for one of Monaco's pioneer real-estate agencies (founded 1942, Chambre Immobilière member). Nine locales with translated URL slugs, daily property sync from the Immotoolbox API, per-property PDF brochures rendered with Typst, multi-currency display refreshed against ECB rates, and a YouTube content feed.
case_gare_challenge_subtitle: A pioneer Monaco agency needed a site as international as its buyers.
case_gare_challenge_1_title: Nine languages, with URL slugs that actually translate
case_gare_challenge_1: Monaco's real-estate buyers come from everywhere — France, Italy, the Nordics, Russia, the UK. A `?lang=` switch isn't credible. Each locale needed its own indexed URL tree with translated path segments.
case_gare_challenge_2_title: Listings live in Immotoolbox, not in the CMS
case_gare_challenge_2: Properties, districts, buildings — all owned by the agency's existing tooling. The site had to pull from the Immotoolbox API on a schedule, store a normalised local copy, and survive the API going down.
case_gare_challenge_3: A serious buyer expects a per-property PDF — generated, cached, regenerated when the listing changes. With nine locales, that's nine PDFs per property. wkhtmltopdf wasn't going to cut it.
case_gare_solution_subtitle: Four interlocking systems, all running on a single Rails monolith.
case_gare_solution_1: French served at `/` (the default), other locales at `/en`, `/it`, `/de`, `/sv`, `/no`, `/da`, `/fi`, `/ru`. URL segments translate per locale — `/ventes/monaco` in French becomes its German equivalent in German. hreflang + per-locale sitemap.
case_gare_solution_2: `bin/rails immotoolbox:sync` pulls districts, buildings and properties from the Immotoolbox API and normalises them into local tables. Runs on a schedule via Solid Queue, idempotent, with Sentry-backed retries — so a flaky API morning never takes the site down.
case_gare_solution_3: Each property has a multi-page PDF brochure generated by Typst — a modern typesetting engine, faster and more legible than LaTeX. Pre-cached on property publish, regenerated on update, served instantly.
case_gare_solution_4: Buyers see prices in their currency. A daily cron job hits the Frankfurter API (ECB rates), caches the result, and powers a currency switcher across the site. No external paywalled rate provider.
case_gare_stack_subtitle: A Rails monolith — boring on purpose, easy to operate.
case_gare_results_subtitle: A multilingual real-estate platform that punches well above its agency size.
case_gare_results_1_value: 9 with translated slugs and per-locale sitemaps
case_gare_results_2_value: Immotoolbox outages don't take the public site down
case_gare_results_3_value: noindex + blocked crawlers + no analytics, gated by an env var
case_gare_byline_title: Built and shipped by me, end-to-end.
case_gare_byline: From the locale routing to the Immotoolbox sync to the Typst PDF pipeline to the Kamal deploy — every layer was designed and written by me. The agency works with one person.
case_gare_cta_title: Need a multilingual real-estate or property site for Monaco?
case_gare_cta_body: I build serious Monaco-aware web platforms — properly indexed, properly translated, properly maintained.
case_cca_meta_title: Christian Curau Architecte — Editorial site + custom web FTP | Laurent Curau
case_cca_meta_description: Sober editorial portfolio for a heritage Monaco architect, plus a stateless web FTP/FTPS client for sharing CAD files securely without a desktop tool.
case_cca_title: Christian Curau Architecte — heritage portfolio + a custom web FTP.
case_cca_lede: Two deliverables for a heritage Monaco architect (founded 1984, Conservateur du Palais Princier). A sober, project-led editorial site that lets the work speak. And a stateless web FTP/FTPS client (CCA-FTP) so the firm can share large CAD files securely from any browser — no desktop tool, no database, encrypted-cookie sessions only.
case_cca_challenge_subtitle: Heritage to convey, large files to move, no in-house IT to maintain it.
case_cca_challenge_1_title: An editorial site, not an agency template
case_cca_challenge_1: A 40-year-old cabinet doesn't need parallax, animated stats, or chatbot pop-ups. It needs a quiet, project-led portfolio that lets the architecture do the talking.
case_cca_challenge_2_title: Sharing 200MB CAD files across email is broken
case_cca_challenge_2: The firm's clients and partners ship large CAD files. Desktop FTP tools are a non-starter (different OS, different network setups). Dropbox/Drive are not options when the file belongs in the firm's own infrastructure.
case_cca_challenge_3_title: No database, no credentials to leak
case_cca_challenge_3: Whatever I built had to be operable by a small architectural team — not by a sysadmin. Storing FTP credentials in a database was a non-goal: a stolen database becomes a stolen passport to every client folder.
case_cca_solution_1: A sober Rails site centred on project pages. Each project carries location, programme, year, and a full-bleed image set. Navigation is restrained, typography does the heavy lifting, and the order of projects on the homepage is curated, not chronological.
case_cca_solution_2_title: CCA-FTP — a stateless web FTP client
case_cca_solution_2: A separate Rails app that bridges any browser to an FTP/FTPS server. Username with `@` routes to FTP, without `@` routes to FTPS — auto-selected, never asked. Credentials live in encrypted cookies for the session only. No database. Stolen disk = nothing useful.
case_cca_solution_3: Filename sanitisation, executable extension blocking, an invisible CAPTCHA against drive-by abuse. Browsing, uploading and downloading work the same way on a desktop and on a phone.
case_cca_solution_4: Both apps deploy via Kamal — single command, zero-downtime, rollback in seconds. Thruster sits in front for HTTP/2. The architect's team doesn't see any of this — they see a URL that works.
case_cca_stack_subtitle: Two Rails apps, both designed to be quiet.
case_cca_results_subtitle: A heritage cabinet, online and operating without IT support.
case_cca_results_1_value: Every project page is a self-contained editorial unit
case_cca_results_2_value: Clients receive a URL, log in, work — no install
case_cca_results_3_value: No DB to back up, no admin to maintain, no on-call
case_cca_byline_title: Built and shipped by me, end-to-end.
case_cca_byline: Both the editorial site and the web FTP were designed, written and deployed by me. Two Rails apps, one engineer, zero hand-offs.
case_cca_cta_title: Need an editorial site or an internal tool — or both?
case_cca_cta_body: I build portfolios for firms that take themselves seriously, and the quiet tools that sit behind them.
